title: Erratic behaviour of muxed signed ports
body:
[test_mux.cpp.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20609126/test_mux.cpp.txt)
[test_mux.sv.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20609127/test_mux.sv.txt)

Hi,

I've created a mux module as follow:
```
module test_mux (
  input clock,
  input reset,
  input signed [23:0] in0,
  input signed [23:0] in1,
  input signed [23:0] in2,
  input signed [23:0] in3,
  input signed [23:0] in4,
  input signed [23:0] in5,
  input signed [23:0] in6,
  input signed [23:0] in7,
  input signed [3:0] sel,
  output signed [23:0] out
);
```

Nothing fancy, just routing `inX` to `out` depending of the value of `sel`.

I'm filling the inputs as follow:

```
    top->in0 = -1;
    top->in1 = 2;
    top->in2 = 3;
    top->in3 = 4;
    top->in4 = 5;
    top->in5 = 6;
    top->in6 = 7;
    top->in7 = 8;
```
I made a simple program to scan the inputs by increasing the `sel` port. Here is the output:

```
-1
255
3
4
5
6
7
8
```

I was expecting:
```
-1
2
3
4
5
6
7
8
```

It looks like the data following a negative input has his last byte turned all to 1.

I've attached the files to reproduce the bug (please remove .txt). You can run it with the following command:
`verilator --cc --exe --build -Wall test_mux.cpp test_mux.sv`

I'm using the following version:
`Verilator 5.036 2025-04-27 rev v5.036-48-g0dc93c1d5`

But this bug is also in 5.006 (Debian packages)

Using Debian 12 (bookworm)

Best regards,
Kevin
code_blocks:
module test_mux (
  input clock,
  input reset,
  input signed [23:0] in0,

  input signed [3:0] sel,
  output signed [23:0] out
);
    top->in0 = -1;
    top->in1 = 2;
    top->in2 = 3;
    top->in3 = 4;
    top->in4 = 5;
    top->in5 = 6;
    top->in6 = 7;
